summary: Fair soundboard capture of an odd‑sounding 1969 show with a fiery Hard To Handle and hot Other One, but plagued by muddiness and tape cuts in several tracks.
review: The performance offers a few standout moments – a jam‑filled Hard To Handle and a scorching version of The Other One – yet the setlist feels unusual for the year and some songs (Slewfoot, Silver Strings) are regarded as weak. Recording quality is decent overall but suffers from muddier sections, a nasty cut on the Pig’s BB Man segue and a garbled ending to Lovelight.

ai_rating.rationale: Multiple reviewers provide consistent comments: show is average with a couple of highlights, recording is decent but has notable flaws; weighting yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Muddy compared to adjacent nights, nasty cut on the Pig’s BB Man intro, tape drop and garbled ending on Lovelight; otherwise a decent capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: An atypical summer‑tour setlist for ’69, missing the usual Dark Star > St. Stephen > The Eleven sequence and featuring several rarely‑played tunes that make the flow feel odd.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned mediating a picket line before the show and praised for his steel‑pedal guitar work; no specific playing critique beyond that.
